prompt: Implement a TradeLoop Offline Marketplace & Moderation Console for a secondhand marketplace that supports end-to-end browsing, messaging, transaction handling, fulfillment, and governance without any network dependency. The application defines four roles: Regular User (buyer/seller), Moderator (content review and policy enforcement), Support Agent (complaints and SLA processing), and Administrator (configuration, permissions, and analytics). Regular Users can register and log in with a username and password, manage a profile (display name, avatar, short bio), and maintain a shipping address book with full CRUD, a single default address, and form rules aligned to US addresses (e.g., ZIP must be 5 digits; state required; phone stored as (###) ###-####). A dedicated User Center surfaces order-style transaction history and refund/complaint outcomes, plus notification preferences for in-app message alerts and moderation outcomes. The secondhand marketplace includes private message threads tied to a listing, with seller/buyer actions that move a deal through explicit statuses: inquiry → reserved (30-minute hold timer) → agreed → completed, with cancellation allowed from inquiry/reserved/agreed and requiring a selectable reason code. Users can block or report another user from a thread; blocked users cannot initiate new threads and existing threads become read-only. Delivery and address management supports pickup or delivery per listing, validates service area by matching ZIP prefixes against a locally managed coverage table, and enforces capacity/time windows (e.g., max 8 deliveries per 2-hour window; windows shown in 12-hour time). Content and Media Management covers CRUD for listings, comments, and Q&A with rich text, images, and short videos, supports categories/tags, pin/featured flags, and versioning with rollback to any of the last 10 saved versions; moderation includes a pre-screen step (local sensitive-word rules) followed by human review with violation tags and a closed loop from report → decision → penalty. The system is built as a Vue.js pure frontend SPA with responsive layout, client-side routing, and complex UI state handled via a centralized store (e.g., transaction state, unread counts, review queues) to keep views consistent across Tabs, Drawers, Modals, and Table-based admin screens. All data persists locally using IndexedDB as the primary “database” for users, listings, threads, addresses, and audit events, with small preferences (theme, notification toggles) in LocalStorage; administrators can export/import full snapshots and filtered reports using browser-side Blob/FileSaver to CSV/Excel-compatible files. RBAC is enforced entirely in the UI and service layer with section/action permissions (e.g., “Content:Delete”, “Reports:Export”), and sessions use an in-browser token with idle timeout at 30 minutes and absolute timeout at 12 hours. Login risk controls include rate limiting (max 5 failed attempts per 10 minutes per username) and lockout (15 minutes), plus password rules (min 12 characters, at least 1 upper, 1 lower, 1 number, 1 symbol) and an offline recovery flow using pre-configured security questions stored as salted hashes. Analytics and data management provide a local data dictionary and category manager, KPI calculations (post volume, claim rate, average handling time) from IndexedDB event timestamps, and trend dashboards rendered from time-series aggregates computed in a frontend service layer over mock/local data. Sensitive fields (password hashes, security answers) must be stored using Web Crypto–based hashing and key derivation, and user-facing screens should mask personal data where practical (e.g., partial phone display) while keeping the app fully functional offline.
